key_insight: A transformer with a tailored encoding can learn to generate representative fine, regular, and star triangulations of 4D reflexive polytopes, including via self-retraining on its own outputs.
engineering_relevance: The result suggests sequence models can tackle highly combinatorial geometry generation problems that are difficult for classical algorithms and useful for Calabi-Yau classification workflows.
method: Encode triangulations into a transformer-friendly representation
Train the model to generate FRSTs across multiple polytope sizes
Retrain on model-generated samples to improve generation quality
evidence_signal: The abstract reports successful representative generation across a range of polytope sizes and that the models can self-improve through retraining on their own output.
watch_item: Performance may depend strongly on the chosen encoding and on whether generated triangulations remain valid and diverse rather than collapsing to a narrow subset.
